message: I've been thinking about how much easier it is to handle emergencies when you've actually thought through the basics ahead of time—no panic, just a plan. Ready.gov has this solid resource for walking through exactly that: what matters to your family, who to contact, where to go. The whole thing takes maybe an hour, and honestly, it feels good to have it done. What's one thing you'd want to make sure your family knows in case you couldn't be reached?
